apple.note:
He is eating a red apple.
An apple a day keeps the doctor away.

book.note:
I read a book every week.
You need to book your flight in advance.

cat.note:
The cat is sleeping on the sofa.
She has a black cat.

water.note:
Please give me a glass of water.
I water the flowers every morning.

happy.note:
He looks very happy today.
Happy birthday to you!

house.note:
They live in a big house.
This house was built in 1990.

school.note:
Children go to school on weekdays.
He is the headmaster of the school.

friend.note:
She is my best friend.
They have been friends for years.

family.note:
I love my family very much.
We are a happy family.

learn.note:
I want to learn English.
She is learning how to drive.

father.note:
My father is a doctor.
He looks just like his father.

mother.note:
Her mother cooked a delicious dinner.
She became a mother of two.

milk.meaning: n. 牛奶; v. 挤奶
milk.note:
I drink milk every morning.
He is milking the cows.

bread.note:
Would you like some bread?
They buy fresh bread daily.

sleep.meaning: v. 睡觉; n. 睡眠
sleep.note:
I need to sleep early tonight.
He is fast asleep.

animal.note:
A dog is a loyal animal.
They went to the zoo to see wild animals.

beautiful.note:
What a beautiful sunset!
She sang a beautiful song.

climate.note:
This region has a warm climate.
The local climate is dry.

driver.note:
The bus driver was very polite.
He is a safe driver.

expensive.note:
That car is too expensive for me.
Eating out in this city is expensive.

health.note:
Exercise is good for your health.
He was restored to full health.

journey.note:
It was a long and tiring journey.
Have a safe journey!

market.meaning: n. 市场; vt. 营销
market.note:
I bought some fresh vegetables at the market.
They are trying to market their new product.

nature.note:
We should live in harmony with nature.
It is his nature to be quiet.

opinion.note:
In my opinion, this plan is perfect.
I would like to hear your opinion.

popular.note:
Soccer is a very popular sport.
She is the most popular girl in class.

science.note:
He is interested in science.
Science has changed our lives.

temperature.note:
The temperature dropped below freezing last night.
She has a high temperature.

useful.note:
This dictionary is very useful.
He gave me some useful advice.

village.note:
He grew up in a small fishing village.
The whole village was flooded.

ability.note:
She has the ability to manage this project.
His ability to speak four languages is impressive.

business.note:
She is in the fashion business.
None of your business.

challenge.meaning: n. 挑战; vt. 向...挑战
challenge.note:
Learning a new language is a great challenge.
She challenged me to a game of chess.

decision.note:
It was a difficult decision to make.
Have you come to a decision yet?

environment.note:
We must protect the environment.
A pleasant working environment is important.

future.note:
What are your plans for the future?
He has a bright future ahead of him.

government.note:
The government announced a tax cut.
He works for the local government.

history.note:
She is studying European history.
This church is rich in history.

improve.note:
I want to improve my speaking skills.
The weather is starting to improve.

knowledge.note:
He has a wide knowledge of history.
Knowledge is power.

method.note:
We need to find a better method.
This method has been proven effective.

necessary.note:
Is it necessary to sign the form now?
Take only what is necessary.

prevent.note:
Vaccines prevent diseases.
Nothing can prevent me from going.

reduce.note:
We must reduce our expenses.
The price was reduced by twenty percent.

society.note:
These changes will benefit society as a whole.
She is a member of the local historical society.

analysis.note:
The expert performed a detailed analysis of the market.
Further analysis is needed to confirm the results.

benefit.meaning: n. 利益;津贴; vt. 有益于; vi. 得益
benefit.note:
The new law will benefit low-income families.
He derived great benefit from the exercise.

consequence.note:
You must accept the consequences of your actions.
The error had serious consequences.

distribute.note:
The organizer distributed pamphlets to the crowd.
Profits are distributed among shareholders.

estimate.meaning: vt. 估计;估算; n. 估价
estimate.note:
It is difficult to estimate the cost of the project.
We accepted the contractor's estimate.

function.meaning: n. 功能;职责; vi. 起作用
function.note:
The main function of the liver is to filter blood.
The machine stopped functioning properly.

hypothesis.note:
Our findings support the original hypothesis.
He formulated a hypothesis to explain the phenomenon.

indicate.note:
A red sky at night often indicates good weather.
The arrow indicates the direction of flow.

lecture.meaning: n. 演讲;讲课; vi. 演讲;讲评
lecture.note:
He gave an interesting lecture on modern art.
She will lecture on biochemistry tomorrow.

maintain.note:
They managed to maintain their lead in the race.
The company maintains that its products are safe.

negotiate.note:
The union is trying to negotiate a new contract.
We managed to negotiate a better price.

obtain.note:
Further information can be obtained from the office.
He obtained a degree in computer science.

policy.note:
The government is reviewing its economic policy.
Honesty is the best policy.

require.note:
This job requires a lot of patience.
Students are required to attend all lectures.

source.note:
The sun is our primary source of energy.
She refused to name her sources.

advocate.meaning: vt. 提倡;主张; n. 倡导者;拥护者
advocate.note:
Many experts advocate for reform in education.
She is a strong advocate of free trade.

bias.meaning: n. 偏见;偏向; vt. 使有偏见
bias.note:
The report was accused of political bias.
We must prevent bias from affecting our decision.

compromise.meaning: n. 妥协;折中; vi. 妥协; vt. 危害
compromise.note:
After hours of discussion, we finally reached a compromise.
We cannot compromise on safety standards.

depict.note:
The movie depicts the hardships of war.
The paintings depict local landscapes.

exploit.meaning: vt. 剥削;开发;利用; n. 业绩;英勇行为
exploit.note:
They are accused of exploiting child labor.
We need to exploit our natural resources wisely.

fluctuate.note:
Oil prices fluctuate wildly from day to day.
My weight fluctuates between 60 and 62 kilograms.

guarantee.meaning: vt. 保证;担保; n. 保证书;担保物
guarantee.note:
I guarantee that you will be satisfied with the service.
The watch comes with a one-year guarantee.

hierarchy.note:
She rose quickly through the corporate hierarchy.
There is a strict hierarchy in the military.

incentive.note:
The government offers tax incentives to small businesses.
There is little incentive for them to work harder.

justify.meaning: vt. 证明...是正当的;替...辩护
justify.note:
How can you justify such high expenditure?
The results justify the costs.

manipulate.note:
She knows how to manipulate people to get what she wants.
He manipulated the clay into a bowl.

novelty.note:
The novelty of the new toy soon wore off.
Electric cars are no longer a novelty.

paradox.meaning: n. 悖论;自相矛盾的人或事
paradox.note:
It is a paradox that light can behave as both a wave and a particle.
He is a paradox: a quiet man who loves noisy parties.

reconcile.meaning: vt. 使和解;调和;使一致
reconcile.note:
It is difficult to reconcile these two opposing views.
They finally reconciled after a long argument.

subtle.meaning: adj. 微妙的;细微的;敏锐的
subtle.note:
There is a subtle difference between the two colors.
He gave a subtle hint that he was not pleased.

anomaly.note:
A temperature of 30 degrees in winter is a weather anomaly.
We are trying to explain this scientific anomaly.

cacophony.meaning: n. 嘈杂的喧嚣声;刺耳的杂音
cacophony.note:
The city streets were a cacophony of car horns and shouting.
A cacophony of protest greeted the politician's speech.

dichotomy.note:
There is a false dichotomy between science and art.
She analyzed the dichotomy of nature and nurture.

ephemeral.meaning: adj. 短暂的;瞬息即逝的
ephemeral.note:
Fame is often ephemeral and easily forgotten.
Spring flowers are beautiful but ephemeral.

garrulous.meaning: adj. 喋喋不休的;饶舌的
garrulous.note:
The garrulous old man talked to anyone who would listen.
She became quite garrulous after a few glasses of wine.

idiosyncrasy.note:
Wearing mismatched socks was one of his idiosyncrasies.
Every language has its own idiosyncrasies.

juxtapose.meaning: vt. 并置;并列以作对比
juxtapose.note:
The exhibition juxtaposes modern art with traditional sculptures.
By juxtaposing the two stories, the author highlights the contrast.

laconic.meaning: adj. 简洁的;言简意赅的;冷漠的
laconic.note:
He sent a laconic reply: 'No.'
His laconic manner made him seem distant.

malleable.meaning: adj. 有延展性的;易受影响的
malleable.note:
Gold is a highly malleable metal.
Young minds are malleable and easily influenced.

nefarious.note:
They conspired in a nefarious scheme to cheat the public.
His nefarious deeds were eventually exposed.

obfuscate.meaning: vt. 使模糊;使混乱;故意使人糊涂
obfuscate.note:
She tried to obfuscate the issue with irrelevant statistics.
Do not use jargon to obfuscate the truth.

pragmatic.note:
We must take a pragmatic approach to solve this crisis.
She is a pragmatic politician who values results over ideology.

quintessential.meaning: adj. 最典型的;最具代表性的
quintessential.note:
He is the quintessential British gentleman.
This cottage is the quintessential country home.

recalcitrant.meaning: adj. 顽抗的;不驯服的;难对付的
recalcitrant.note:
The recalcitrant horse refused to jump the fence.
Staff members were frustrated by recalcitrant students.

scintillating.meaning: adj. 闪烁的;才华横溢的;生动有趣的
scintillating.note:
She gave a scintillating performance on stage.
The conversation was scintillating and full of wit.

taciturn.meaning: adj. 沉默寡言的;不苟言笑的
taciturn.note:
He was a quiet, taciturn man who rarely spoke.
Her taciturn nature was often mistaken for coldness.

ubiquitous.meaning: adj. 无处不在的;普遍存在的
ubiquitous.note:
Smartphones have become ubiquitous in daily life.
Fast food chains are ubiquitous in this country.

vacillate.meaning: vi. 犹豫不决;摇摆;波动
vacillate.note:
He vacillated between the two job offers for weeks.
Her opinions vacillate according to who she is speaking to.

winnow.note:
The selection committee will winnow down the list of candidates.
We must winnow the truth from the rumors.

zealot.note:
He was labeled a religious zealot.
Only a zealot would agree with such extreme measures.

abundant.note:
The region is abundant in natural resources.
They had an abundant supply of food for the winter.

coincide.meaning: vi. 同时发生;相符;一致
coincide.note:
My holiday plans coincide with yours.
The interests of the two companies coincide.

diverse.meaning: adj. 各种各样的;不同的;多元的
diverse.note:
The student body is very culturally diverse.
We hold diverse opinions on this subject.

evidence.meaning: n. 证据;迹象; vt. 证明
evidence.note:
There is no evidence to support his claim.
The police are gathering evidence against the suspect.

fluency.note:
One of the requirements for the job is fluency in French.
She spoke with great fluency and confidence.

genuine.meaning: adj. 真实的;真诚的;真正的
genuine.note:
Is this a genuine leather jacket?
He showed a genuine interest in our project.

hostile.meaning: adj. 敌对的;敌方的;不友好的
hostile.note:
They received a hostile reception from the crowd.
The environment is extremely hostile to life.

influence.meaning: n. 影响;势力; vt. 影响
influence.note:
My parents had a major influence on my career choice.
Don't let him influence your decision.

jealous.note:
She was jealous of her sister's success.
He gets jealous when she talks to other men.

limit.meaning: n. 限制;限度; vt. 限制;限定
limit.note:
There is a strict speed limit here.
We must limit the number of attendees to fifty.

motivate.meaning: vt. 激发;刺激;作为...的动机
motivate.note:
How do you motivate your staff to work harder?
Fear of failure can motivate us to try harder.

notion.note:
He has a vague notion of what he wants to do.
She challenged the traditional notion of marriage.

obstacle.note:
Lack of funding is the main obstacle to our progress.
She overcame many obstacles to achieve her dream.

predict.links: predicts, predicted, predicting, prediction, predictable, unpredictable
predict.note:
It is difficult to predict the future accurately.
Scientists predict that the volcano will erupt soon.

quote.meaning: vt. 引用;引述;报价; n. 引文;报价
quote.note:
Can you quote a price for this repair work?
He quoted a passage from Shakespeare.

reject.note:
The publisher rejected his new novel.
She rejected his offer of marriage.

secure.meaning: adj. 安全的;可靠的; vt. 获得;保护
secure.note:
Please make sure your seatbelt is secure.
He managed to secure a loan from the bank.

tolerate.links: tolerates, tolerated, tolerating, tolerance, tolerant, tolerable
tolerate.note:
I cannot tolerate this noisy environment any longer.
We must learn to tolerate different opinions.

urgent.note:
This is an urgent matter that needs immediate attention.
We received an urgent message from headquarters.

vivid.meaning: adj. 生动的;鲜艳的;清晰的
vivid.note:
He gave a vivid description of his journey.
I have a vivid memory of my first day at school.

wonder.meaning: vi. 想知道;感到惊讶; n. 惊奇;奇迹
wonder.note:
I wonder why he didn't show up.
It is a wonder that he survived the crash.

youth.note:
He spent his youth traveling the world.
She was a famous athlete in her youth.

zone.note:
This is a smoke-free zone.
They divided the city into different commercial zones.

yield.meaning: vt. 产生;屈服;让步; n. 产量;收益
yield.note:
The investment yields high returns.
He refused to yield to their demands.

xenophobia.meaning: n. 惧外;排外;仇外心理
xenophobia.note:
The rise of nationalism has led to increased xenophobia.
We must work together to combat racism and xenophobia.

withstand.note:
The building was designed to withstand strong earthquakes.
He could not withstand the pressure from his peers.

vulnerable.meaning: adj. 易受伤害的;脆弱的;有弱点的
vulnerable.note:
Older people are more vulnerable to the flu.
The city is vulnerable to sea-level rise.

unprecedented.meaning: adj. 空前的;前所未有的
unprecedented.note:
The country is experiencing unprecedented economic growth.
This level of rain is unprecedented in recent history.

trivial.meaning: adj. 微不足道的;琐碎的;无价值的
trivial.note:
Don't waste your time on such trivial matters.
It seemed a trivial objection at the time.

scrutinize.note:
The bank will scrutinize your financial records before approving the loan.
Every detail of the contract was closely scrutinized.

redundant.meaning: adj. 多余的;过剩的;被解雇的
redundant.note:
The computer system has redundant servers in case of failure.
He was made redundant after the company downsized.

prerequisite.meaning: n. 先决条件;前提; adj. 必备的
prerequisite.note:
A high score on the test is a prerequisite for admission.
This course is a prerequisite for advanced chemistry.

paradigm.note:
The study represents a major paradigm shift in physics.
He is considered a paradigm of professional integrity.

obsolete.meaning: adj. 淘汰的;废弃的;过时的
obsolete.note:
New technology has made the old machines obsolete.
Gas lamps became obsolete after the invention of electric lights.

meticulous.meaning: adj. 一丝不苟的;周密的;小心翼翼的
meticulous.note:
She is meticulous about keeping her room clean.
He made a meticulous plan for the expedition.

lucrative.meaning: adj. 获利丰厚的;赚大钱的
lucrative.note:
He runs a very lucrative export business.
The merger proved to be highly lucrative for both companies.

jeopardize.note:
His careless actions could jeopardize the whole mission.
Do not jeopardize your health by working too hard.

inherent.meaning: adj. 固有的;内在的;与生俱来的
inherent.note:
There is an inherent risk in any financial investment.
Love of freedom is inherent in human nature.

grandiose.meaning: adj. 宏伟的;华而不实的;浮夸的
grandiose.note:
He has grandiose plans for starting a multinational company.
They live in a grandiose mansion with thirty rooms.

formidable.meaning: adj. 可怕的;难对付的;令人敬畏的
formidable.note:
The champion proved to be a formidable opponent.
They faced a formidable task of rebuilding the city.

exacerbate.note:
The cold weather will only exacerbate his joint pain.
His aggressive comments exacerbated the tension.

diversity.note:
The biology class studied the diversity of life on Earth.
There is a great diversity of opinions on this issue.

cognitive.note:
We studied the cognitive development of young children.
Her cognitive skills remained sharp even in her nineties.

bureaucracy.meaning: n. 官僚主义;官僚机构
bureaucracy.note:
We must cut through the government bureaucracy to get things done.
Many businesses complain about excessive bureaucracy.

alleviate.note:
A warm bath can help alleviate muscle tension.
The government introduced measures to alleviate poverty.

benevolent.note:
A benevolent neighbor offered to look after our dog.
The organization was founded by a benevolent businessman.

aesthetic.meaning: adj. 美学的;审美的; n. 审美观
aesthetic.note:
The building was designed with both practical and aesthetic considerations.
She has a highly developed aesthetic sense.

synthesize.note:
He tried to synthesize the results of several different studies.
This laboratory can synthesize new compounds.

resilient.meaning: adj. 有弹性的;能迅速恢复的;适应力强的
resilient.note:
She is a resilient girl who quickly recovers from setbacks.
The local economy proved resilient to the global crisis.

profound.meaning: adj. 深奥的;渊博的;极度的
profound.note:
The book had a profound influence on my thinking.
He expressed profound sympathy for the victims.

opulent.meaning: adj. 豪华的;奢侈的;富裕的
opulent.note:
They lived an opulent lifestyle in a luxury penthouse.
The hotel is decorated in an opulent style.

metamorphosis.note:
A caterpillar undergoes metamorphosis to become a butterfly.
The company went through a complete metamorphosis under the new CEO.

gregarious.meaning: adj. 社交的;群居的;爱交际的
gregarious.note:
She is a gregarious person who loves going to parties.
Sheep are highly gregarious animals.

esoteric.meaning: adj. 秘传的;深奥难懂的;限于少数人的
esoteric.note:
The specialist published a paper on an esoteric aspect of physics.
He has an esoteric interest in ancient languages.

dilatory.note:
The government was criticized for its dilatory response to the crisis.
His dilatory tactics delayed the progress of the negotiations.

connoisseur.note:
He is a connoisseur of fine French wines.
She is an art connoisseur who works for a gallery.

belligerent.meaning: adj. 好战的;交战的;好斗的; n. 交战国
belligerent.note:
The country adopted a belligerent foreign policy.
His belligerent attitude led to a fight.

acquiesce.meaning: vi. 默许;默认;勉强同意
acquiesce.note:
She chose to acquiesce in her husband's decision.
We cannot acquiesce to their unfair demands.

capricious.meaning: adj. 反复无常的;善变的;任性的
capricious.note:
The weather in this area is notoriously capricious.
She was guided by capricious whims rather than reason.

dogmatic.meaning: adj. 武断的;教条的;独断的
dogmatic.note:
He was so dogmatic that he refused to listen to anyone else.
She rejected his dogmatic assertions about religion.

equivocal.meaning: adj. 模棱两可的;可疑的;含糊的
equivocal.note:
His political record is somewhat equivocal.
He gave an equivocal answer that satisfied no one.

fastidious.meaning: adj. 挑剔的;过分讲究的;苛求的
fastidious.note:
She is fastidious about her personal appearance.
He is a fastidious planner who leaves nothing to chance.

iconoclast.meaning: n. 特立独行的人;反传统的人;打破旧习者
iconoclast.note:
As an iconoclast, he challenged all established artistic rules.
She was seen as an iconoclast in the field of politics.

loquacious.meaning: adj. 多话的;话多的;喋喋不休的
loquacious.note:
The loquacious host kept the guests entertained for hours.
He became quite loquacious after drinking a glass of wine.

obsequious.meaning: adj. 谄媚的;奉承的;卑躬屈膝的
obsequious.note:
The manager was surrounded by obsequious assistants.
He resented their obsequious behavior towards the boss.

perfidious.meaning: adj. 背信弃义的;不忠实的
perfidious.note:
We were betrayed by a perfidous ally.
He condemned their perfidious behavior.

superfluous.meaning: adj. 多余的;过剩的;累赘的
superfluous.note:
The writer cut several superfluous paragraphs from the article.
Your explanation is helpful but superfluous.

vociferous.meaning: adj. 大声疾呼的;喧嚷的
vociferous.note:
He was a vociferous critic of the government's economic policies.
The proposal met with vociferous opposition.
